title: Success Story: An Indian Senior Controls Enginee Achieves EB-1A Approval in Just 16 Days
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
"Thanks for getting this through, appreciate all the efforts and quick turnarounds.”
On March 18 th , 2026, we received another EB-1A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ) approval for a Senior Motor Control Engineer in the Field of Control of Electrical Drives ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Control of Electrical Drives
Position at the Time of Case Filing: Senior Motor Control Engineer
Country of Origin: India
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: California
Approval Notice Date: March 18 th , 2026
Processing Time: 16 days (Premium Processing Requested)
We recently represented a Senior Motor Control Engineer from India working in the field of control of electrical drives. His work centers on intelligent motor drive technologies, with particular emphasis on sensorless rotor position estimation, torque optimization, and self-commissioning strategies for synchronous machines. Although highly technical, these areas have clear practical value. Advances in motor control help make electrified transportation, industrial automation, robotics, and renewable-energy systems safer, more efficient, and more reliable.
What made this case especially strong was the combination of originality and real-world relevance. Our client’s work did not stay confined to theory. His research addressed the performance, stability, and efficiency of motor-drive systems in ways that other experts in the field were already using and building upon.
A Record of Achievement That Stood Out
The evidence showed a sustained pattern of accomplishment. Our client had authored 11 first-authored journal articles and 13 peer-reviewed conference articles, 12 of them first-authored. His work had also drawn broad international attention, with citations from researchers in at least 43 countries. He ranked among the top 1% most highly cited authors publishing on motor control topics over the past 10 years, and his h-index of 13 placed him among the top 3% of authors in electronic engineering.
Contributions That Helped Define the Field
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) highlighted several areas where our client’s work had made a meaningful mark.
One major contribution involved improving the efficiency and reliability of complex control systems. By developing novel estimation techniques that reduced the need for physical hardware dependencies, this work supported more cost-effective and robust system designs. Another important area involved enhancing dynamic control and operational stability in advanced machinery. The client developed methods that allowed for highly accurate performance metrics even under fluctuating operating conditions—advances that are highly valuable in settings requiring precise control.
The client also advanced automated calibration and monitoring methods, achieving exceptional accuracy. These improvements supported more efficient system deployment, stronger fault detection, and better long-term operational performance.
Recognition from the Field
This case was also strengthened by substantial evidence of peer recognition. Our client had completed at least 91 peer reviews for major journals in the field and had been selected as a star reviewer. These are meaningful signals of trust, showing that other experts and leading journals recognized his authority and expertise.
This approval reflected the strength of a record built on original contributions, sustained recognition, and practical influence in a highly important engineering field. We are delighted that we could help present this extraordinary case successfully.
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
Track Record of Success EB-1 and NIW Approvals
